target: build-scripts/build-doc-v4.js — but THIS FILE NO LONGER EXISTS in current main. Commit 438b1f7 'Remove DOCX build infrastructure: build-doc-v2/v3/v4.js, adm-zip, docx deps' (2026-04-07T17:18:18+01:00, author Devilwench, co-authored Claude Opus 4.6) deleted build-doc-v2.js (416 lines), build-doc-v3.js (793 lines), build-doc-v4.js (898 lines), removed adm-zip and docx dependencies from package.json, and deleted the legacy DOCX/PDF artifacts under downloads/. The HNOTE that commissioned §B2 was authored 2026-04-24T18:30:00Z — 17 days after the target file was deleted. The premise of §B2 ('update build-doc-v4.js to use the same font stack as the HTML build') is therefore obsolete.
kernel_of_truth: Operator's underlying intent is real and correct: the downloadable artifact that readers print or save should match the on-screen typographic identity established by EXP-202/207 (Source Serif 4 body, Inter UI, JetBrains Mono code, all self-hosted under docs/fonts/, no third-party runtime requests per NC8). Typographic parity between the canonical screen view and the canonical export view is a legitimate Phase B polish target. The kernel survives even though the literal target file (build-doc-v4.js) does not, because the same parity question can be asked of whatever export pipeline currently exists.
finding_summary: PHASE B2 IS SCOPE-OBSOLETE AS WRITTEN. The DOCX export pipeline was removed in V5.0 (commit 438b1f7, 2026-04-07). The current downloadable artifact is downloads/critical-review-dome-model-v6.pdf, generated by build-scripts/generate-pdf.js, which uses Playwright headless Chromium to load docs/index.html (file:// URL) and emit a PDF via page.pdf(). Because Playwright's Chromium renders the same HTML that ships to readers — including the @font-face block at generate-html.js:495 and the --serif/--sans/--mono CSS variables at generate-html.js:496 — the PDF inherits the Source Serif 4 / Inter / JetBrains Mono stack automatically, with no separate font configuration. The typographic-parity goal of §B2 is already structurally guaranteed by the architecture, not by an editable font declaration in a DOCX builder. There is no work for §B2 to do as commissioned.

git_commit_that_deleted_target.author: Devilwench <67862852+Devilwench@users.noreply.github.com>
git_commit_that_deleted_target.subject: Remove DOCX build infrastructure: build-doc-v2/v3/v4.js, adm-zip, docx deps
git_commit_that_deleted_target.body: Cleanup after V5.0 migration to HTML→PDF. Updates CLAUDE.md to reflect new architecture: Playwright for PDF, sections.json as sole prose source, no more DOCX generation or LibreOffice dependency.

current_export_pipeline.mechanism: Playwright headless Chromium loads docs/index.html via file:// URL, uses @media print CSS, emits via page.pdf({format:'Letter',printBackground:true,margin:...})
current_export_pipeline.relevant_lines: build-scripts/generate-pdf.js:25 (page.goto with waitUntil:'networkidle' guarantees fonts load before printing); :29-37 (page.pdf invocation)

html_font_block_already_canonical.print_media_block_inherits: generate-html.js:645 (@media print:root) — same --serif/--sans/--mono tokens, so the print pathway uses identical fonts to the screen pathway. No separate print font stack to drift from.

package_json_no_docx_deps.verdict: No 'docx' npm package, no 'adm-zip' (which was used for the bookmark-ID workaround), no 'jszip', no 'officegen' — nothing that would build a .docx. Confirms removal is complete.

fonts_directory_in_main.reachable_from_pdf_pipeline: Yes — Playwright loads docs/index.html via file:// and the @font-face URLs are relative ('./fonts/...woff2'), so they resolve against docs/index.html's directory. waitUntil:'networkidle' in generate-pdf.js:25 ensures the woff2 files are downloaded into the rendering context before page.pdf() snapshots the page. Chromium then embeds the rendered glyphs into the PDF.
fonts_directory_in_main.pdf_inherits_html_typography: Yes, by construction — there is one HTML, one font block, one print stylesheet, one Chromium rendering it twice (interactive view and PDF snapshot). No mechanism for the screen and PDF to diverge unless someone introduces one.

fuse_workspace_has_orphan_copy.observation: The FUSE mount /sessions/zen-nice-sagan/mnt/dome-model-review/ retains a stale build-scripts/build-doc-v4.js dated Apr 7 06:03 (118762 bytes). FUSE cannot unlink, so the deletion in commit 438b1f7 never propagated to the workspace. This file is NOT under git tracking on origin/main; it is workspace cruft. Anyone editing it would be editing a phantom that the publish pipeline ignores.
fuse_workspace_has_orphan_copy.implication: Even if a future analyst (or a rushed operator action) wrote font edits into the FUSE copy of build-doc-v4.js, the change would never reach git or the published site. The orphan file is a fork-in-the-road trap, not a viable target.

recommendation.primary: MARK §B2 OBSOLETE / NO-ACTION. Update HNOTE-001's consumed_progress.B2 to status='scope_obsolete' with this EXP as the artifact. Decider does NOT need to queue a curmudgeon review (there is no patch to review). Decider does NOT need to assign a follow-up EXP — there is no underlying defect to fix.
recommendation.secondary: If the operator's true intent was to ALSO ship a DOCX export (in addition to the existing PDF), that is a substantially larger commission than 'typography alignment': it requires (a) re-adding the docx + adm-zip dependencies, (b) restoring or rewriting build-doc-v4.js (or a successor) to consume sections.json, (c) re-implementing the bookmark-ID workaround that the V5.0 cleanup specifically removed (per SESSION-CONTEXT.md:201, the docx-js package sets all bookmark IDs to 0 and adm-zip was used to regex-replace them with sequential IDs), (d) re-establishing the build target and sync paths in build.js. None of those belong in 'Phase B polish'. They are a Phase C+ project that needs its own HNOTE with explicit operator scoping.
recommendation.tertiary: Phase B sequencing impact: HNOTE-001 specified B1 + B2 first, B3 last. With B2 obsolete, the recommended next item is B3 (design-system prefix unification, EXP-251 slot). The HNOTE's stated rationale for B3-last ('B1 and B2 ship first so B3 has a stable final class set to rename once') is unaffected by this finding — B1 has shipped, B2 contributes no new classes, so B3 already has a stable class set.

what_no_action_means_concretely: No edit to build-scripts/ — build-doc-v4.js does not exist to edit.
No new files in docs/fonts/ — the OTF/TTF variants the HNOTE asked for are unnecessary because no Word/DOCX consumer exists.
No package.json changes — adm-zip and docx remain absent, as intended by V5.0.
No build.js changes — there is no DOCX target to register.
No tests changed — none were dependent on a DOCX path.
No visual delta in HTML or PDF outputs — the existing PDF (downloads/critical-review-dome-model-v6.pdf) already renders Source Serif 4 / Inter / JetBrains Mono via the existing pipeline.

acceptance_criteria.AC1: Decider closes EXP-250 in the tracker with status='complete', integrated=true (the 'integration' here is just acknowledging the finding; there is no patch to apply).
acceptance_criteria.AC2: HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001 consumed_progress.B2 is updated to status='scope_obsolete' (or operator-preferred terminology) with delivery_note pointing at this EXP and the 438b1f7 commit.
acceptance_criteria.AC3: No commit modifies build-scripts/, docs/fonts/, package.json, or build.js as a result of B2.
acceptance_criteria.AC4: If the operator wants to revisit DOCX export as a separate project, a new HNOTE is filed with explicit scope (which deps to re-add, what bookmark-ID strategy to use, what build target wiring is required). This EXP does NOT preclude that decision; it just declines to do it under the §B2 banner.
acceptance_criteria.AC5: Phase B sequencing continues: B3 (prefix unification, EXP-251) becomes the next analyst Mode 2 target after B2 is closed. No B2-revival item is created.

anticipated_objections.objection: But the HNOTE clearly said to do the work. Shouldn't the analyst just RESTORE build-doc-v4.js and proceed?
anticipated_objections.response: No. The HNOTE explicitly delegates scope-question authority to the analyst: 'Flag any scope questions back via a new analyst issue-proposal; do not block on waiting.' Restoring an 898-line generator and two npm dependencies that were deliberately deleted in a named architecture migration (V5.0) is not 'Phase B polish' — it is a reversal of an architectural decision that the operator/team explicitly made and committed under signed authorship. The bar for reversing a named-version architectural decision is an explicit HNOTE that says 'restore DOCX,' not an oblique reading of a typography-alignment HNOTE that assumed the file still existed.

anticipated_objections.objection: What if the operator just forgot V5.0 happened?
anticipated_objections.response: Plausible — and that is the most likely explanation given the 17-day gap between the deletion (Apr 7) and the HNOTE (Apr 24) plus the fact that the HNOTE mentions 'build-doc-v4.js' by exact name, suggesting the author was working from older context. This EXP names the gap explicitly so the operator can decide on the right next step (revive DOCX as a separate commission, or accept that PDF parity is already achieved). The analyst's job is to surface the discrepancy, not silently round it to either 'do the work anyway' or 'ignore the HNOTE.'

anticipated_objections.objection: But §B2 says 'or rely on Word's font-substitution' — can we just document the substitution behavior and call it done?
anticipated_objections.response: No, because there is no Word-consumed file produced anywhere in the build. Documenting Word's substitution policy for a file that does not exist is theater, not analysis. If we ship a finding, it should be the actual finding (file deleted; PDF inherits HTML fonts via Chromium) so the operator can decide whether the underlying intent is satisfied.

anticipated_objections.objection: The PDF inherits HTML fonts only if Playwright actually loads them. Has that been verified?
anticipated_objections.response: Mechanism is well-defined: page.goto(file://docs/index.html, {waitUntil:'networkidle'}) waits for all subresources — including @font-face URLs — to settle before resolving. The @font-face declarations use relative URLs ('./fonts/source-serif-4-variable.woff2') that resolve against the file:// base, so the woff2 files load from the local docs/fonts/ directory. Chromium then renders text using the loaded fonts and embeds the rendered glyphs into the PDF via page.pdf(). This is the standard Playwright PDF mechanism. A direct visual confirmation against downloads/critical-review-dome-model-v6.pdf opened in any PDF reader is the cheap acceptance test if the curmudgeon wants belt-and-suspenders. (One byte-level note: the embedded font streams in a Chromium-emitted PDF are FlateDecode-compressed, so a naive grep of the raw PDF for 'Source Serif' or 'Inter' will return no hits — that is normal and not evidence of font absence.)

anticipated_objections.objection: Are there any non-Word DOCX consumers we'd be neglecting? Pandoc, LibreOffice, mobile readers?
anticipated_objections.response: Not as published. The site's published downloads in main are exactly one PDF (critical-review-dome-model-v6.pdf). There is no .docx in downloads/ on origin/main since the V5.0 cleanup. Any reader who needs DOCX today is generating it themselves from the PDF or HTML, which is not a workflow the project ships or supports. Adding such a workflow is the separate-project recommendation in this EXP, not a §B2 task.

anticipated_objections.objection: The HNOTE budget tracker assumed three EXPs. Skipping one breaks the count.
anticipated_objections.response: EXP-250 IS the third EXP — it just delivers a finding instead of a code patch. Tracker integrity is preserved (id=EXP-250, status=complete, output_file points at this JSON). The HNOTE's deliverable spec ('Three EXP files in monitor/analyst/expansions/') is met; it does not require all three to ship code. The same HNOTE explicitly authorizes scope-question issue-proposals as an alternative deliverable path.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_: Why not silently leave §B2 incomplete and proceed to B3? Because workspace-sync would push a stale 'pending' status that would re-trigger Mode 2 on the next analyst run, and the next analyst would hit the same wall and have to redo this analysis. A persistent 'scope_obsolete' marker prevents the loop.

Why not file this as an issue (open-issues.json) instead of an EXP? Because the HNOTE commissioned three EXPs and the decider's accounting expects three EXPs. Issues-only would create a cross-tracker mismatch. The EXP+issue-proposal pair preserves both expectations.

Why include the FUSE orphan observation? Because anyone investigating this finding from the workspace mount will see build-doc-v4.js sitting there and assume it's live code. The observation pre-empts that confusion.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC1: No third-party runtime dependencies introduced — no work was done that could introduce any.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC2: No regression to HTML output — no HTML changes proposed.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC3: No regression to PDF output — no PDF generator changes proposed.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC4: No regression to test suite — no test changes proposed.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC5: Phase B boundary preserved — this EXP stays inside §B2 and does not creep into B1, B3, or HNOTE-002 territory.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC6: Decider's auto-integrate-after-curmudgeon clearance still applies — this finding can clear curmudgeon trivially since there is no code change to review.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC7: OFL license obligations unchanged — no font files added, removed, or modified.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC8: Same-origin posture preserved — no new external assets proposed.

out_of_scope_for_this_exp: Reviving DOCX export — explicit recommendation to file as a separate HNOTE if the operator wants that.
Sourcing OTF/TTF font variants — premise was DOCX-driven; no consumer for them.
Editing the FUSE-orphan build-doc-v4.js — would be invisible to git and is therefore a non-action.
Refactoring generate-pdf.js — out of scope for typography-alignment.
Changing the HTML font stack — out of scope; B2 was about export-alignment, not screen typography.
Touching B1 or B3 territory — explicitly bounded by HNOTE-001 §B1/§B3 boundaries.
notes_for_human_review_group: The kernel here is the gap between the HNOTE's mental model (a DOCX export pipeline still exists and needs typographic alignment) and the actual repo state (DOCX export removed in V5.0, two and a half weeks before the HNOTE was authored). The gap is recoverable cheaply — close §B2 as obsolete, optionally file a fresh HNOTE if DOCX revival is genuinely wanted — and the rest of Phase B is unaffected (B1 already shipped; B3 is sequencing-stable). The cost of NOT flagging this is high: a future analyst either silently does the wrong thing (writes font edits into a phantom file or re-creates a deleted generator) or burns another full Mode 2 run rediscovering the same finding. Either failure mode wastes Opus capacity for no benefit. If you (operator) believe DOCX export should return, please file a new HNOTE with explicit scope: which dependencies to restore, which sections.json consumer to write or restore, which bookmark-ID strategy to use, and what the acceptance test for typographic parity is on the DOCX side. That is a much bigger commission than 'Phase B polish' implies.

related_exps: EXP-202 (Phase A foundation — self-hosted WOFF2 webfont introduction; this is the CSS state §B2 would have aligned to)
EXP-207 (Phase A — first HTML consumer of the webfont stack; canonical reference for what 'aligned typography' means)
EXP-249 (Phase B1 — pie chart cleanup; sibling EXP from the same HNOTE)
Future: EXP-251 (Phase B3 — design-system prefix unification; recommended next Mode 2 target)

new_evidence_added: Commit 438b1f7 (2026-04-07) shown to delete build-doc-v4.js (898 lines), build-doc-v3.js (793 lines), build-doc-v2.js (416 lines), adm-zip + docx package deps, and legacy DOCX/PDF artifacts.
Current package.json verified to contain only 'playwright' as a dependency.

new_value.delivery_note: Target file build-scripts/build-doc-v4.js was deleted in commit 438b1f7 (2026-04-07), 17 days before HNOTE was authored. Current PDF pipeline (build-scripts/generate-pdf.js via Playwright) inherits HTML font stack automatically; typographic parity already achieved by architecture. No code change. Recommend closing §B2 obsolete and proceeding to §B3 (prefix unification) on next Mode 2 run.

tldr_evidence: build-scripts/build-doc-v4.js was deleted in commit 438b1f7 on 2026-04-07 along with adm-zip and docx npm deps. The HNOTE that asked us to update it was written 17 days later. Current PDF is generated by Playwright headless Chromium loading the same HTML the reader sees, so it already uses Source Serif 4 / Inter / JetBrains Mono via the @font-face block at generate-html.js:495 — typographic parity is structural, not editorial.
tldr_verdict: §B2 is scope-obsolete. No code change needed; recommend closing it as 'obsolete' and proceeding to §B3 on the next Mode 2 run. If DOCX export should genuinely return, that's a separate HNOTE-sized commission, not a Phase B polish task.
